text.p: parts of Lower South East district.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while storms are nearby.

warning-info.text: The next warning is due to be issued by 4:10 am.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: HEAVY RAINFALL WITH THUNDERSTORMS IN THE SOUTHEAST

text.p: Weather Situation: A low pressure trough is producing thunderstorms with heavy rainfall over the far southeast.

Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Mount Gambier, Robe and Millicent.
Robe Airport recorded 28mm in the hour to 12:45 am

Severe thunderstorms are likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding in the warning area over the next several hours. Locations which may be affected include Mount Gambier, Robe and Millicent.
Robe Airport recorded 28mm in the hour to 12:45 am
